get.description: • Function description: Query the account list and return account information.

parameters.name: x-app-key
parameters.description: A unique identifier issued to a developer for accessing an application's API.

parameters.name: x-app-secret
parameters.description: A unique key issued to developers to access the application's API.

parameters.name: x-timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp of the request, follows ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, e.g. 2023-07-16T19:23:51Z, only supports UTC time zone.

parameters.name: x-signature-version
parameters.description: Signature algorithm version, default is 1.0.

parameters.name: x-signature-algorithm
parameters.description: Signature algorithm, default is HMAC-SHA1.

parameters.name: x-signature-nonce
parameters.description: Signature unique random number.

parameters.name: x-access-token
parameters.description: An access token is a credential that represents the authorization granted to a client (e.g., a user or an application) to access specific protected resources on behalf of a user, without needing to share their password.

parameters.name: x-version
parameters.description: Interface version, Accepts only the value v2.

parameters.name: x-signature
parameters.description: A signature is a unique digital fingerprint, typically encrypted, that verifies the authenticity and integrity of a message or transaction, ensuring it has not been tampered with during transmission.

417.description: A business logic error triggered when the request cannot be processed due to domain-specific constraints.

post.description:
• Function description: Query Token status.This API is used to check the validity of a given token. If the status is NORMAL, the token is active and can be used normally. If the status is PENDING, the token is pending verification and requires a mobile verification code via the Webull App. If the status is INVALID, the token is invalid and must be regenerated. If the status is EXPIRED, the token has expired and must be regenerated.
• Frequency limit: Rate limit 10 requests every 30 seconds

token.description: Access token string, used for authentication of subsequent API calls. Token is a 32-digit hexadecimal string that is unique and time-sensitive.

expires.description: Token expiration timestamp, a Unix timestamp in milliseconds. After this time, the token will become invalid and need to be recreated.

status.description: Token validity status code, indicating the result of the token operation. PENDING indicates pending verification, NORMAL indicates valid, INVALID indicates the token is invalid, and EXPIRED indicates it has expired.

schema.description: Token response information
schema.title: TokenRespVo

post.description:
• Function description: Create an access token.This interface is used to generate a new Token, which is the credential for accessing other API interfaces. Upon successful creation, it returns a response containing Token information, expiration time, and status. The Token status defaults to 'Pending Verification' and requires verification via Webull App SMS code. Tokens are time-sensitive (default 15 days) and need to be refreshed before expiration.
• Frequency limit: Rate limit 10 requests every 30 seconds

get.description:
• Function description: Query the recent N bars of data based on stock symbol, time granularity, and type. Supports historical bars of various granularities like M1, M5, etc. Currently, daily bars (D) and above only provide forward-adjusted bars; minute bars provide unadjusted bars.
• Frequency limit: Market-data Interfaces Rate limit 60 requests every 60 seconds

parameters.name: symbol
parameters.description: Security symbol.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Security type. Category values are as shown in the enum; US_OPTION type query is currently not supported.

parameters.name: timespan
parameters.description: Bar time granularity.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of bars, default 200, maximum limit 1200 (M1 supports up to 1650).

parameters.name: real_time_required
parameters.description:
Return the latest trading data, default is true;
true: Pulls only the completed bars from the previous period at the nearest whole hour at the time of request.
false: The returned data includes the latest market data.

parameters.name: trading_sessions
parameters.description: Specify trading hours. Multiple selections are allowed. Separate multiple items with ",".

Supports historical bars of various granularities like M1, M5, etc. Currently, daily bars (D) and above only provide forward-adjusted bars; minute bars provide unadjusted bars.
• Frequency limit: Market-data Interfaces Rate limit 60 requests every 60 seconds
(Required) A unique identifier issued to a developer for accessing an application's API.
(Required) A unique key issued to developers to access the application's API.
(Required) Timestamp of the request, follows ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, e.g. 2023-07-16T19:23:51Z, only supports UTC time zone.
(Required) Signature algorithm version, default is 1.0.
(Required) Signature algorithm, default is HMAC-SHA1.
(Required) Signature unique random number.

(Required) An access token is a credential that represents the authorization granted to a client (e.g., a user or an application) to access specific protected resources on behalf of a user, without needing to share their password.
(Required) Interface version, Accepts only the value v2.

(Required) A signature is a unique digital fingerprint, typically encrypted, that verifies the authenticity and integrity of a message or transaction, ensuring it has not been tampered with during transmission.

symbols.description: List of security symbols, supports JSON array format, multiple symbols separated by commas; maximum 100 symbols per query.

items.description: List of security symbols, supports JSON array format, multiple symbols separated by commas; maximum 100 symbols per query.

category.description: Security type. Category values are as shown in the enum;

count.description: Number of bars, default 200, maximum limit 1200 (M1 supports up to 1650).

real_time_required.description:
Return the latest trading data, default is true;
true: Pulls only the completed bars from the previous period at the nearest whole hour at the time of request.
false: The returned data includes the latest market data.

trading_sessions.description: Specify trading session(s). Multiple sessions separated by ",".

• Function description: Query the most recent N footprint records based on stock symbol, and category, time granularity.•
• Frequency limit: Market-data Interfaces Rate limit 600 requests per minute

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: List of security symbols, supports JSON array format, multiple symbols separated by commas; maximum 20 symbols per query.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Security type. Category values are as shown in the enum; Only US_STOCK type queries are supported.

parameters.name: timespan
parameters.description: Supports granularities such as S5, S15, M1, M5, and M30.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of bars, default 200, maximum limit 1200.

parameters.name: real_time_required
parameters.description: Does it include the latest data? For candlesticks that are not yet finalized, the default is false (does not include). Only minute timespan is used.

parameters.name: trading_sessions
parameters.description: Specify trading hours. OVN type not supported.

parameters.name: x-app-key
parameters.description: A unique identifier issued to a developer for accessing an application's API.

parameters.name: x-app-secret
parameters.description: A unique key issued to developers to access the application's API.

parameters.name: x-timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp of the request, follows ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, e.g. 2023-07-16T19:23:51Z, only supports UTC time zone.

parameters.name: x-signature-version
parameters.description: Signature algorithm version, default is 1.0.

parameters.name: x-signature-algorithm
parameters.description: Signature algorithm, default is HMAC-SHA1.

parameters.name: x-signature-nonce
parameters.description: Signature unique random number.

parameters.name: x-access-token
parameters.description: An access token is a credential that represents the authorization granted to a client (e.g., a user or an application) to access specific protected resources on behalf of a user, without needing to share their password.

parameters.name: x-version
parameters.description: Interface version, Accepts only the value v2.

parameters.name: x-signature
parameters.description: A signature is a unique digital fingerprint, typically encrypted, that verifies the authenticity and integrity of a message or transaction, ensuring it has not been tampered with during transmission.

symbol.description: Security symbol

total.description: The sum of the main buy and sell volumes

delta.description: The difference in trading volume (primary buyers - primary sellers)

additionalProperties.description: The main purchase footprint details (quantity combined for items with the same price).

buy_detail.description: The main purchase footprint details (quantity combined for items with the same price).

additionalProperties.description: The main seller's footprint shows details (quantities combined for the same price).

sell_detail.description: The main seller's footprint shows details (quantities combined for the same price).

417.description: A business logic error triggered when the request cannot be processed due to domain-specific constraints.

get.description:
• Function description: Get real-time market snapshot data for a security. Returns key market indicators such as latest price, price change, volume, turnover rate, etc. Supports querying various security types including US stocks, with optional inclusion of pre-market, after-hours, and overnight trading data.
• Frequency limit: Market-data Interfaces Rate limit 60 requests every 60 seconds

parameters.name: symbols
parameters.description: List of security symbols, supports JSON array format, multiple symbols separated by commas; maximum 100 symbols per query.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Security type. Category values are as shown in the enum; US_OPTION type query is currently not supported.

parameters.name: extend_hour_required
parameters.description: Whether to include pre-market and after-hours trading data.

parameters.name: overnight_required
parameters.description: Whether to include overnight trading data.

parameters.name: x-app-key
parameters.description: A unique identifier issued to a developer for accessing an application's API.

parameters.name: x-app-secret
parameters.description: A unique key issued to developers to access the application's API.

parameters.name: x-timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp of the request, follows ISO8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ, e.g. 2023-07-16T19:23:51Z, only supports UTC time zone.

parameters.name: x-signature-version
parameters.description: Signature algorithm version, default is 1.0.

parameters.name: x-signature-algorithm
parameters.description: Signature algorithm, default is HMAC-SHA1.

parameters.name: x-signature-nonce
parameters.description: Signature unique random number.

parameters.name: x-access-token
parameters.description: An access token is a credential that represents the authorization granted to a client (e.g., a user or an application) to access specific protected resources on behalf of a user, without needing to share their password.

parameters.name: x-version
parameters.description: Interface version, Accepts only the value v2.

parameters.name: x-signature
parameters.description: A signature is a unique digital fingerprint, typically encrypted, that verifies the authenticity and integrity of a message or transaction, ensuring it has not been tampered with during transmission.

symbol.description: Trading symbol of the financial instrument.Represents the unique identifier of the security in the specified market (e.g., ticker symbol for equities).

open.description: Open price, for US stocks it's intraday open price, excluding pre/post market data. No return value if no trading occurred on the day

high.description: Today's high price, for US stocks it's intraday high, excluding pre/post market data. No return value if no trading occurred on the day

low.description: Today's low price, for US stocks it's intraday low, excluding pre/post market data. No return value if no trading occurred on the day

volume.description: Volume. No return value if no trading occurred on the day

change.description: Change amount. No return value if no trading occurred on the day

• Function description: Order details, query the specified order details through the order ID.
• Frequency limit: Rate limit 40 requests every 2 seconds

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Account identifier.

parameters.description:
Unique client-defined identifier for the order.
Maximum length is 32 characters and must be unique per account.
Used to track or reference the order when interacting with the system.

parameters.description: A unique key issued to developers to access the application's API.

parameters.description: Signature algorithm version, default is 1.0.

parameters.description: Signature algorithm, default is HMAC-SHA1.

parameters.name: x-signature-nonce
parameters.description: Signature unique random number.

parameters.description: An access token is a credential that represents the authorization granted to a client (e.g., a user or an application) to access specific protected resources on behalf of a user, without needing to share their password.

parameters.description: Interface version, Accepts only the value v2.

parameters.description: A signature is a unique digital fingerprint, typically encrypted, that verifies the authenticity and integrity of a message or transaction, ensuring it has not been tampered with during transmission.

client_order_id.description:
Client-defined order identifier. Returned in the response for simple orders.
Represents the unique order ID assigned by the user when placing the order.

combo_type.description:
Type of order combination.
• NORMAL: Indicates a standard single order.

client_order_id.description:
Client-defined order identifier. Returned in the response for simple orders.
Represents the unique order ID assigned by the user when placing the order.

order_id.description:
System-generated order identifier. Returned in the response for simple orders.
Represents the unique Webull order ID assigned by the system.

symbol.description: Trading symbol of the financial instrument.Represents the unique identifier of the security in the specified market (e.g., ticker symbol for equities).

side.description: The order side indicating the intended trading direction of the transaction. The meaning of side may vary depending on the instrument_type and account type (e.g., margin vs. cash).

status.description:
• PENDING: Indicates that the order has been submitted to the exchange and is awaiting completion
• SUBMITTED: Indicates that the order has been submitted to the exchange and is awaiting completion

• CANCELLED: Indicates that the order has been successfully cancelled
• FILLED: Indicates that the order has been fully executed
• FAILED: Indicates a failed order, such as REJECTED
• PARTIAL_FILLED: Refers to the portion of the order that has been completed, but not all of it has been completed

order_type.description:
Specifies the type of order to be placed. Determines how the order will be executed in the market.
Available order types depend on the market and instrument type.
U.S. Stock
  • MARKET: Market Order
  • LIMIT: Limit Order
  • STOP_LOSS: Stop Order
  • STOP_LOSS_LIMIT: Stop Limit Order

instrument_type.description: Type of financial instrument associated with the request.

support_trading_session.description:
Specifies the trading session for the order. Applicable to U.S. stock market orders only.
Active values:
• NIGHT: Only supports night trading.
• ALL: Include extended trading hours.
• CORE: Only support regular trading hours.
• ALL_DAY: Included Overnight Hours, 8:00 p.m.ET - 8:00 p.m.ET(the next day)

time_in_force.description:
Specifies the duration for which the order remains active in the market (Time-In-Force).
• DAY: The order is valid only for the current trading day and expires at the end of the day.
• GTC: Good-Till-Canceled, the order remains active until it is executed, explicitly canceled, or reaches the maximum allowed duration (typically 90 days).

entrust_type.description:
Specifies the method for placing the order.
• QTY: Order specified by quantity of shares or units.
• AMOUNT: Order specified by total cash amount, applicable for fractional share trading of US stocks.

total_quantity.description: Total order quantity. Represents the total number of units submitted for this order.

filled_quantity.description: Quantity that has been executed. Represents the number of units that have been filled so far.

filled_price.description: Average transaction price of the filled quantity. If the order has not been executed yet, this may be zero or null.

limit_price.description:
Limit price of the order. Required when order_type is LIMIT, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT.
Specifies the maximum (for buy) or minimum (for sell) price at which the order can be executed.

stop_price.description:
Stop price of the order. Required when order_type is STOP_LOSS, STOP_LOSS_LIMIT.
Specifies the trigger price at which the stop order becomes active.

place_time.description: Order placement time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.

place_time_at.description: Order placement time in ISO8601 format (UTC). Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ

filled_time.description: Time of the last executed trade in milliseconds since Unix epoch.

filled_time_at.description: Time of the last executed trade in ISO8601 format (UTC). Format: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ
